notes: With-skill achieved 100% pass rate across all 22 assertions (9+5+8). Without-skill passed only 6/22 (27.3%).
Assertion 'Includes slv_c_utils.h as the primary SDK header' passes in both configurations (3/3 each) — non-discriminating, consider removing.

Assertions 'Uses c_sysary or c_sysfnc' and 'Uses c_errmes' partially pass without-skill (2/3 and 1/3 respectively) — the baseline model knows these function names exist but uses them incorrectly or inconsistently.

The most discriminating assertions are: struct-based C signature (0% without skill), iflag guard (0% without skill), ev_PRIVATE_EVENT handling (0% without skill), dflag awareness (0% without skill), and PAR[] parameterization (0% without skill).
Without-skill consistently falls back to a Fortran-style flat parameter signature with trailing underscore — this is the single biggest correctness issue the skill fixes.

The forbidden-call-trap eval shows partial baseline knowledge: the model knows c_sysary exists and mentions the caching alternative, but still presents the forbidden approach as the primary recommendation.
Timing/token data was not captured for these runs (subagent notifications were not processed). Consider re-running with timing capture for a complete benchmark.
